sections.content:
Hmm, clicking Left V button 1 didn't change anything. Left V stayed "ggwggggwggggwggggwgg". Maybe I clicked the wrong spot. Let me look at the V button layout more carefully.
The V buttons are at row 47. The pattern is "ggwggggwggggwggggwgg".
Each button group is 5 chars wide: ggwgg, ggwgg, ggwgg, ggwgg.
The 'w' is at position 2 within each group. So the clickable part of each V button is at col 8, 13, 18, 23 for Left V.
I clicked row 47, col 8, which should be the 'w' of the first Left V button. But nothing changed.
Wait, maybe the V buttons are already ON and clicking them turns them OFF. But the pattern didn't change.
Let me try clicking a different part of the V button, like the top or bottom.
Actually, looking at the image, the V buttons are vertical switches. The clickable part might be the top or bottom of the switch, not the center.
Let me try clicking row 45 (top of the switch) or row 49 (bottom of the switch).
Actually wait - I just realized something. The V buttons are VERTICAL switches. The H buttons are HORIZONTAL switches. The V buttons span multiple rows, not just one row. Let me look at the ASCII more carefully.
Left V buttons:
- Row 39-43: T-shaped tops (black)
- Row 47: ggwggggwggggwggggwgg (the switch bodies)
- Row 51: I-shaped bottoms (light gray)
So the V buttons are vertical structures spanning rows 39-51. The clickable part might be the top (row 39-43) or the bottom (row 51).
Let me try clicking the top of a V button.

print("\nRight V button area (rows 39-51):")

</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, now I see the V button structure more clearly. Let me look at the Left V buttons:
- Row 39: gwwwggwwwggwwwggwwwg → top bar
- Row 41-43: ggwggggwggggwggggwgg → vertical stem
- Row 45: gwwwggwwwggwwwggwwwg → middle bar
- Row 47-49: ggwggggwggggwggggwgg → vertical stem
- Row 51: BBBBBBBBgBBBBBBBBBBB → bottom bar (black)
And the Right V buttons:
- Row 39: wwwWWwwwWWwwwWWwwwWW → top bar
- Row 41-43: WwWWWWwWWWWwWWWWwWWW → vertical stem
- Row 45: wwwWWwwwWWwwwWWwwwWW → middle bar
- Row 47-49: WBWWWWBWWWWBWWWWBWWW → vertical stem
- Row 51: BBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBBB → bottom bar (black)
Wait, I see that the Left V buttons have 'w' (light gray) in the vertical stems, and the Right V buttons have 'w' or 'B' (black) in the vertical stems.
Looking at the Right V buttons more carefully:
- Row 47: WBWWWWBWWWWBWWWWBWWW
- The pattern is: W B W W W W B W W W W B W W W W B W W W
- Split into 4 groups: WBWWW, WBWWW, WBWWW, WBWWW
- Each group has B at position 1.
So the Right V buttons have B (black) in the stem, which might mean they're OFF. And the Left V buttons have w (light gray) in the stem, which might mean they're ON.
But when I clicked row 47, col 8 (which is the 'w' of the first Left V button), nothing changed. Maybe I need to click a different part of the button.
Let me try clicking the top bar of a V button, like row 39, col 8.
Actually, wait. Let me re-examine the H buttons.
Left H:
- Row 33: gBBBggBBBggBBBggBBBg → the button tops (black = OFF)
- Row 37: gwwwggwwwggwwwggwwwg → the button bottoms (light gray = ON?)
Right H:
- Row 33: wwwWWBBBWWBBBWWBBBWW → button 1 is www (ON), rest are BBB (OFF)
- Row 37: wwwWWwwwWWwwwWWwwwWW → all www (light gray)
Hmm, the H buttons have two rows: row 33 (top) and row 37 (bottom). When I clicked row 33, col 35 (first Right H button), it changed from BBB to www. So clicking the top of an H button toggles it.
For V buttons, the structure is more complex. Let me try clicking the top bar (row 39) of a V button.
